lived.dateLabel: Hilary Term 1468 Common Pleas debt action
lived.summary: WAALT CP40/826 indexes Michael Conygrave as plaintiff in a Hertfordshire debt action in Hilary Term 1468; the named defendants were of St Albans, Hertford, Panshanger, and Hertford.

people.notes: Michael remains the earliest display-ready Conygraue spelling and the first known Hertfordshire property-fine actor in this spelling cluster, but he is no longer the earliest exact Conygrave-form person after the 1455 John Conygrave of St Albans Common Pleas find. Michael is attested as Michael Conygraue in a Hertfordshire final concord on 1 May 1457 and as Michael Conygrave in a Hertfordshire Common Pleas debt action in Hilary Term 1468. The 1457 fine supports Westminster court context and a Hertfordshire property cluster at Stapleford, Bengeo, and Datchworth; it does not prove that Michael resided in every property place. No parish or family details have been extracted. John Conygrave of St Albans in 1455 is a stronger local antecedent/kinsman lead than the Staffordshire Richard bridge, but no source proves that John was Michael's father. Thomas Conygrave of London/Westminster in 1490 is retained only as a possible later southeast-cluster continuation.

father.notes: No source names Michael's father. Richard Congreve in the 1440 and 1455 Staffordshire Common Pleas indexes is a weak earlier-generation bridge only by chronology and surname proximity, not a proven pedigree link. John Conygrave of St Albans in 1455 is a local antecedent/kinsman lead, but he is not added as a father candidate because the index does not establish generation or kinship.

children.notes: No source names Michael's children. Thomas Conygrave of London/Westminster in 1490 is a candidate later southeast-cluster continuation only because of rare spelling, chronology, and Hertfordshire-London proximity.
